key_insight: WeldMamba predicts future weld-pool segmentation from past images and process signals by embedding physics-guided temporal dynamics into a state space model.
engineering_relevance: This enables lookahead perception for closed-loop control in laser wire-feed welding, helping compensate for sensing and actuation delays.
method: Uses historical coaxial grayscale images plus welding parameters and wire-state electrical signals as inputs.
Combines a visual encoder, conditioned normalization, patch-level temporal state space modeling, and horizon-conditioned latent prediction.
Adds motion-aware decoding with signed-distance, temporal consistency, distillation, and keyhole-focused losses to constrain future geometry.
evidence_signal: On a 43-sequence laser welding dataset, the method achieved 74.63% mIoU at a 500 ms lookahead.
watch_item: Performance may depend on accurate alignment of multimodal signals and careful tuning of auxiliary losses for stable deployment.
paper_title: Physics-Guided Spatiotemporal State Space Modeling for Lookahead Molten Pool Segmentation in Laser Wire-Feed Welding
